cases.title: Active session row and Pending attendance remain consistent in PostgreSQL throughout live check-in window
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL test database is running via Compose
Active session exists with status Active, openedAt set, and N enrolled students with Pending attendance rows
SessionService and AttendanceRepository wired to real Postgres adapter
steps: Query sessions table for sessionId and assert status is Active with openedAt not null and closedAt null
Count attendance_records for sessionId with status Pending and assert count equals N
Simulate M successful check-in transactions via CheckInService (M ≤ N) against the Active session
Re-query sessions table and assert status remains Active (no spurious Draft or Closed transition)
Re-query attendance_records and assert M rows are Present and (N − M) remain Pending
cases.expected: Session stays Active with enrolled Pending rows intact during live check-in per NFR-01; no unplanned state corruption or session termination in database

cases.title: 150 parallel check-in and QR poll requests during Active session produce zero 5xx responses
preconditions: Active session with 150 enrolled students and valid rotating QR tokens
API and PostgreSQL running in full-preview or integration test harness
Each virtual user has unique authenticated Student session and in-radius GPS fixture
steps: Start Active session with QR scheduler running
Launch 150 concurrent POST /api/v1/check-in requests staggered over 0–5 minutes
Concurrently poll GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/qr/current at 60 req/min from Instructor context
Collect HTTP status codes for all requests over the Active window
Assert zero responses with status 500, 502, or 503
Assert error rate from non-5xx business rejections (DuplicateCheckIn, ExpiredQr) is below 0.1%
cases.expected: Service remains available under pilot-scale concurrent load per NFR-01 and testing-plan §7; no unplanned server errors during live check-in window

cases.title: GET /api/v1/health returns 200 with status ok and db connected while Active session has enrolled students
preconditions: API running against healthy Compose PostgreSQL
Active session with at least one enrolled student in Pending status exists
steps: Send GET /api/v1/health without authentication
Assert HTTP status 200
Assert response body matches { "status": "ok", "db": "connected" } per 13-docker-compose-local-runtime.md §9
Repeat health poll every 10 seconds for duration of simulated 2-minute Active check-in window
Assert every poll returns HTTP 200 with db connected
cases.expected: Liveness/readiness endpoint confirms service and database reachable throughout live session per NFR-01 and 13-docker-compose-local-runtime.md §9 health contract

cases.title: Critical check-in endpoints return non-5xx for entire 10-minute Active attendance window
preconditions: Active session opened with valid GPS and N enrolled students
Authenticated Instructor and enrolled Student sessions available
TestClock injectable to advance through attendance window without exceeding scheduledStart + 10 minutes
steps: At window start, GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/qr/current with Instructor session and assert HTTP 200
POST /api/v1/check-in with valid token and in-radius coordinates using Student session and assert HTTP 200
Advance TestClock by 5 minutes and repeat GET qr/current and POST check-in for a second student
Advance TestClock to scheduledStart + 9 minutes and repeat both endpoints
Assert no request during the window returns HTTP 500, 502, or 503
cases.expected: QR issuance and check-in submission endpoints remain continuously available during the full Active attendance window per NFR-01 zero-downtime target

cases.title: Flow A workshop lifecycle completes with zero unplanned API outage minutes
preconditions: Full E2E stack running: PostgreSQL, API on port 3001, seeded roster of 100–150 students
Uptime probe script polls GET /api/v1/health every 5 seconds
Instructor and Student test accounts authenticated
steps: Record uptime probe start timestamp
Instructor creates session with GPS, opens session to Active, and begins QR display polling
Students submit check-ins sequentially and in small parallel batches until ≥99% of cohort reaches Present
Instructor closes session after check-in window
Stop uptime probe and compute total seconds where health returned non-200 or db disconnected while session status was Active
Assert computed unplanned downtime equals 0 minutes (0 seconds)
cases.expected: End-to-end workshop check-in per testing-plan Flow A completes with 0 minutes unplanned downtime during Active session per NFR-01 and MVP exit indicator §9

cases.title: TS-13/14: Active session open and in-window check-in remain available without service interruption
preconditions: Draft session with valid room GPS and enrolled roster exists
Authenticated Instructor and enrolled Student
Monitoring captures API availability from open through first successful check-in
steps: POST /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/open and assert HTTP 200 with status Active
Immediately GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/attendance and assert HTTP 200 with pending count equal to enrollment
GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/qr/current and assert HTTP 200 with valid qrPayload
POST /api/v1/check-in within scheduledStart + 10 minutes and assert HTTP 200 outcome Success
Verify no health probe failure occurred between open and successful check-in
cases.expected: Session open (TS-13) and in-window check-in (TS-14) complete without unplanned outage per AC-05 traceability to NFR-01

cases.title: QR token rotation scheduler continues issuing Valid tokens without session state regression during Active window
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with Active session and QR scheduler enabled
TestClock fixed at session openedAt
At least two 30-second rotation cycles within attendance window
steps: Assert session status Active in sessions table
Advance TestClock by 30 seconds and invoke QrService.rotateTokens or wait for scheduler tick
Query qr_tokens for sessionId and assert exactly one Valid token exists
Advance TestClock by another 30 seconds and repeat rotation
Re-query sessions table and assert status remains Active with closedAt null
Assert no gap where zero Valid tokens exist between rotations
cases.expected: FR-06 QR rotation continues uninterrupted during Active session; session never spuriously leaves Active state per NFR-01 availability requirement

cases.title: Instructor QR display page remains responsive with rotating QR throughout Active session
preconditions: Full-preview or dev stack running with web on port 3000 and API healthy
Instructor logged in and navigated to Active session QR display route
Session has enrolled students and attendance window is open
steps: Open instructor session monitor / QR display page for Active sessionId
Assert QR image element is visible and countdown timer displays seconds remaining
Wait 35 seconds without manual page reload
Assert QR image updates to a new token and countdown resets near 30 seconds
Repeat observation at 70 seconds and 105 seconds into Active window
Assert no blank screen, error boundary crash, or persistent loading spinner appears
cases.expected: Instructor-facing QR display stays available and auto-refreshes during live session per NFR-01; zero minutes of UI unavailability blocking check-in

cases.title: Student mobile check-in flow completes confirmation without page failure during Active session
preconditions: Active session with valid rotating QR displayed on instructor screen or test fixture
Student authenticated on mobile viewport (375×667) in browser
Student device location mocked to in-radius coordinates
steps: Student navigates to check-in deep link or scans QR to load check-in route
Assert GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:tokenId/preflight returns HTTP 200 and GPS capture step mounts with session summary per AC-07c
Grant camera mock or use token entry path with in-radius gpsSim fixture and submit check-in
Assert success confirmation message appears in Vietnamese within 2 seconds
Navigate back to personal attendance history and assert session shows Present
Assert no unhandled error toast, white screen, or network-failure banner indicating service outage
cases.expected: Student check-in UI remains functional end-to-end during Active session per NFR-01; no client-visible downtime blocking attendance submission

cases.title: PostgreSQL stop during Active session surfaces 503 health; recovery restores service before session auto-close
preconditions: Active session with enrolled students and attendance window not yet elapsed
Compose db service controllable via docker compose stop/start
Uptime probe logging health responses with timestamps
steps: Confirm GET /api/v1/health returns 200 with db connected while session Active
Stop db container with docker compose stop db
Poll GET /api/v1/health and assert HTTP 503 or response body db disconnected
Attempt POST /api/v1/check-in and assert non-success without data corruption (no partial Present)
Restart db with docker compose start db and wait for pg_isready
Poll health until HTTP 200 db connected returns
Verify session status in database remains Active if recovery occurs before scheduledStart + 10 minutes
Assert check-in succeeds for remaining Pending student after recovery
cases.expected: Dependency failure is observable via health contract per 13-docker-compose-local-runtime.md §9; after recovery within window, service resumes and check-in continues with zero additional unplanned downtime beyond controlled test interruption

cases.title: Rate-limited check-in returns 429 with Retry-After not 5xx preserving service availability during Active session
preconditions: Active session with authenticated Student
Rate limit configured per API design §10.3 (10 requests per student per session within 10 minutes)
Valid QR token and in-radius coordinates available
steps: Submit 10 POST /api/v1/check-in requests within the 10-minute attendance window for same student (expect business rejections after first success)
Submit 11th request within the same 10-minute session window
Assert HTTP status 429 with RateLimitExceeded error envelope and Retry-After header per 09-error-handling §2.6
Assert response is not HTTP 500, 502, or 503
GET /api/v1/health immediately after 429 and assert HTTP 200 db connected
cases.expected: Rate limiting degrades gracefully with 429 and Retry-After per NFR-01 and testing-plan §5 RateLimitCheckIn; service remains up and health endpoint healthy during Active session
